media.altText: Lush green trees frame steep mountain slopes covered in thick mist

sections.subtitle: Primeval Yunnan Forests and Minority Cultures
sections.text: Two days in the Laojun Mountains, far from the Lijiang crowds. We start at Lashi Wetlands at dawn, stand at the First Bend of the Yangtze, visit traditional Naxi, Bai, Pumi and Lisu minority villages, then join a Lisu bonfire dance in the evening and a forest walk at night. Day two is a bird watching station visit and a primeval forest trek to grasslands overlooking the highest peaks of Laojun Mountains. You'll leave with a slower pulse and mountain stories to tell.

items.title: Duration
items.subtitle: Two Days
items.highlight:
Day 1: 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM
Day 2: 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM

items.title: Location
items.subtitle: Laojun Mountains
items.highlight: Laojun Mountains UNESCO Natural Heritage Site | Altitudes 2,400-3,100m

items.title: Activity Level
items.subtitle: Moderate
items.highlight: Hiking: No more than 8-9 km per day

items.title: Panoramic Views
items.subtitle: First Bend of the Yangtze
items.text: Stand in awe at the panoramic grandeur of the Yangtze's First Bend. Discover the unique geography shaping this iconic river and capture unforgettable views from this dramatic vantage point.

image.altText: First Bend of Yangtze River panorama

items.title: Cultural Exchange
items.subtitle: Lisu Culture
items.text: Immerse yourself in the vibrant culture of the Lisu minority through an unforgettable bonfire dance and music experience. Join in the festivities, connect with local traditions, and create lasting memories under the stars.

items.title: Night Walk
items.subtitle: Nocturnal Wildlife
items.text: Awaken your senses on an enchanting night walk through a primeval forest. Discover the hidden wonders of nocturnal wildlife, experience the forest's mystical ambiance, and connect with nature in a unique way.

items.title: Historical Market
items.subtitle: Shigu Town
items.text: Step back in time in charming Shigu Town, a cultural gem of the Naxi minority. Discover the historic relict of Stone Drum, wander through local market, and immerse yourself in the unique history of this quaint town.

items.title: Bird Watching
items.subtitle: Bird Watching Station
items.text: Track the critically-endangered Biet's Laughingthrush at a Laojun Mountain village birding station — one of the highest reliable viewing spots in the world, with 30+ other Yunnan endemics possible in a single morning.

image.altText: Three people in a camouflaged hide using binoculars and a camera

items.title: Sustainable Beekeeping
items.subtitle: Himalayan Bee Farm
items.text: Delve into the fascinating world of Himalayan bee-keeping, a unique local tradition. Discover sustainable practices, learn about the bees' vital role, and savor the exquisite taste of pure, locally sourced honey.

items.title: Cultural Heritage
items.subtitle: Taohua Village
items.text: Discover the idyllic beauty of Taohua, a traditional Bai ethnic minority village. Admire the elegant white pagodas and a historic stone bridge. Immerse yourself in the peaceful rhythm of rural life.

items.title: Alpine Wetlands
items.subtitle: Lashi Nature Reserve
items.text: Witness a breathtaking spectacle of nature at Lashi Wetlands Nature Reserve, a vital haven for diverse waterbirds. Explore this protected reserve, learn about its ecological significance, and marvel at the vibrant tapestry of avian life in a stunning wetland landscape.

items.title: Citizen Science
items.subtitle: Biodiversity Monitoring
items.text: Become a citizen scientist! Learn about cutting-edge biodiversity monitoring techniques, including infrared cameras, and understand vital conservation efforts. Gain hands-on experience and contribute to protecting this unique ecosystem.

items.title: Rural Life
items.subtitle: Liju Village
items.text: Experience the authentic daily life of the Lisu minority in Liju village. Feel their warm hospitality, learn about their sustainable way of living, and gain insights into a culture deeply connected to nature.

items.title: Alpine Meadows
items.subtitle: Laojun Mountains Peaks
items.text: Embark on a rewarding hike with a local conservationist, following a pristine forest stream to a stunning alpine meadow. Be captivated by panoramic views of Laojun Mountains' peaks and learn about the unique alpine ecosystem.

items.title: Ancient Giant
items.subtitle: Natural Wonder
items.text: Marvel at an immense spruce tree, a natural wonder steeped in local lore. Walk inside its hollow trunk, discover its cultural significance, and capture a truly unique photo opportunity within this ancient giant.

activities: Begin with a pickup from your Lijiang hotel and head to the breathtaking Lashi Wetlands and watch elegant waterbirds glide across mirror-still waters as the sun paints the wetlands in golden hues. Stand in awe at the legendary First Bend of the Yangtze River.

Discover the vibrant market of Shigu Town, steeped in Tang dynasty history, Naxi heritage and revolutionary stories. Immerse yourself in the rich traditions of the Bai minority at Taohua village. Admire ancient white pagodas, cross historic stone bridges, and feel the warm hospitality of local villagers. Arrive at your overnight destination deep in the mountain forests to learn infrared camera techniques and contribute to vital biodiversity monitoring.

Savor authentic local cuisine before connecting with Lisu traditions through an enchanting bonfire dance and music experience. Create lasting memories as you join in festivities around the warm bonfire. Take a short stroll into the wild primeval forest at night, hear nocturnal animals and observe rare insects that come out during cool nights. Finally, settle into your comfortable mountain retreat.

days.title: Rare Birds, Old Forests and Mountain Views

activities: Awaken with birdsong and venture into pristine forests at a local bird watching station. Learn from passionate conservationists as you spot some of the world's rarest birds in their natural habitat. Follow a crystal-clear stream into ancient forests. Spot beautiful endemic flowers, medicinal plants and curious mushrooms along the leisurely path. Arrive at alpine meadows with panoramic views of the Laojun Mountains.

After a hearty local BBQ lunch it's time for a desert. A few spoonfuls of pure honey produced by the laborious Himalayan bees from countless species of wild flowers in these primeval forests. Take a peek into the traditional beehives and learn about the vital role the bees play in preserving this rich ecosystem. Before heading back to Lijiang, pay your last respect to the majestically sacred Dragon Spruce tree that stands as a local symbol of natures resilience.
Conclude your journey with a feast of Lijiang specialties in an Old Town restaurant before returning to your hotel full of wild memories.

contactNote.text: For groups of 1 person or more than 6 people

included.text: English-Speaking Coordinator/Guide (Lynne Lyu or Lukasz Madrzynski), Local Guides, Traditional Local Food (Breakfasts, Lunches, Dinners), Comfortable Accommodation in Lisu minority Guesthouse, Private Transportation, Outdoor Travel Insurance.
included.notIncluded: Personal Expenses (Souvenirs, etc.), Voluntary Gratuities for the Coordinators/Guides
included.meetingPoint: Pickup: 9:00 AM at your Lijiang hotel | Return: Approximately 8:00 PM to your Lijiang hotel

sections.subtitle: Click on a question to reveal the answer

items.question: What should I bring?
items.answer: We recommend comfortable hiking shoes, layers for changing temperatures, raincoat or umbrella for rainy days, sun protection, a water bottle, and your camera. A headlamp or flashlight is useful for the night forest walk.

items.question: Is this suitable for children?
items.answer: Yes! This tour is family-friendly. Children under 12 should be accompanied by an adult. The moderate hiking distance (8-9 km/day) is achievable for most fitness levels, and the nature and culture activities are engaging for all ages.

items.question: What's the cancellation policy?
items.answer: Full refund if cancelled 7+ days before the tour. 50% refund for 3-6 days. No refund within 48 hours.

items.question: What happens if it rains?
items.answer: Most activities continue in light rain. For safety during heavy storms, we'll adjust the itinerary to indoor nature and culture activities.

items.question: Are there any physical requirements?
items.answer: Basic fitness is recommended. You'll hike up to 9 km per day on mountain trails. The terrain is generally gentle but at altitude (up to 3,100m), pace will be slower.

items.question: How do I get to the meeting point?
items.answer: We provide pickup from any hotel in Lijiang Old Town area. If you're staying outside this area, please contact us to arrange an alternative meeting point.

items.question: What language do the guides speak?
items.answer: We are fluent in English, Polish and Chinese.

items.question: How does this trip support conservation and sustainable development?
items.answer: Each booking directly supports local conservation efforts and community development. We partner with nature reserves for wildlife monitoring, employ local and Lisu guides and community hosts, contribute a portion of proceeds to the local bird watching station and are actively involved in local environmental protection and sustainability initiatives.

title.text: Ready to Go Deep into Wilderness?

seo.metaTitle: Laojun Wild Heart Tour | Yunnan UNESCO Birding + Trekking Ecotour
seo.metaDescription: Escape to Laojun Mountain, Yunnan, China. Track Biet Laughingthrush at a village birding station, hike alpine meadows, and stay overnight with a Lisu family. Small-group Yunnan eco tour.
